comment: Canonical V3Labs brain-tool catalog. The platform owns this file; every brain framework (jarjar, mcp, function_calling, langchain, ws) consumes from here rather than defining its own. When the patch shape evolves, this is the only place that needs updating — server-side validation reads the same schema the brain was told to emit, so there's no drift between 'what the brain produces' and 'what the platform accepts'.

tools.name: upgrades.propose
tools.description: Propose a UI patch in response to the user's `/upgrade <description>` request. Returns a patch payload the lobby renders as a SOCIAL POST in Genetics: every patch publishes with a Twitter-length caption (≤140 chars, plain user voice, no marketing copy) and 3-5 hashtags drawn from the patch's effect or target surface. You are the dexhero authoring this post on behalf of the user; write the caption the way the user themselves would, not the way a feature release note would. The patch is NOT committed until the user clicks Commit on the card.

description.description: One- or two-sentence summary of the change.

target_surface.description: Which surface the patch primarily targets.

css.description: Sanitized CSS. Use CSS variables when possible. Auto-scoped to the patch region by the applier.

script.description: JS source — runs sandboxed in a Web Worker.

behaviors.description: Optional scripted behaviors. Sandboxed at runtime.

caption.description: Twitter-length social caption (≤140 chars) describing the change in plain user voice — what they'd say if they were posting this to a feed themselves. NOT marketing copy. Examples: "Tighter, blacker, rounder. Popovers quietly disappear into the bg.", "Warmed up the chat replies — easier on the eyes at 2am.", "When you tap a slot it now breathes. Tiny detail, huge vibe.". Required for the social-post framing on Genetics.

tags.description: 3-5 hashtag strings (rendered as #chips on the social card). No leading '#'; lowercase, single-word preferred. Drawn from the patch's effect (darkmode, minimal, glow, terminal…) or surface (popovers, ticker, chat, slots, theme).

proposal_id.description: Client-side id; the real commit lands when the user clicks Commit.
